docs/dev/engineering-principles.md:50:- **Decision generated:** `ddx agent execute-bead` produces a merge commit (or preserved branch ref) rather than rebasing or squashing. docs/helix/00-discover/product-vision.md:54:6. **Context rot is real; bounded context execution is the response.** LLM output quality decays as a single context window fills — well before the hard token limit. Long-running agent sessions accumulate transcript, tool noise, and failed attempts that compete with the original instructions; the agent at hour one is not the agent at minute one. The structural fix is **bounded context execution** — also known as the **Ralph loop**: every unit of work runs in a fresh, narrowly-scoped context against an explicit contract (a bead with acceptance criteria), with persistent state landing on disk as evidence rather than carried forward as transcript. DDx implements this with `ddx agent execute-bead` (single bounded attempt in an isolated worktree) and `ddx work` (queue drain that re-enters the loop with a fresh context per bead). See `website/content/docs/concepts/bounded-context-execution.md` for the full treatment.

.claude/skills/docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:101:## Principle: uniform harness invocation shape, embedded transport for ddx-agent
.claude/skills/docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:105:**ddx-agent should expose the same invocation shape, but stay in-process.** No subprocess required — the agent library is Go and DDx is Go. The reason to make the shape uniform isn't transport; it's so DDx can have a single `Harness` interface in its own code with multiple implementations:
.claude/skills/docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:118:`RunRequest` carries: `Prompt`, `Model`, `WorkDir`, `Permissions`, `Effort`, `Timeout`, `Metadata`. Nothing else. Tools are not in `RunRequest` — claude doesn't take a tools flag, codex doesn't, ddx-agent won't.
.claude/skills/docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:122:Tools are entirely internal to whichever harness is running. `claude` does not let DDx pick its tools; `codex` does not; `ddx-agent` won't either. Permission level is the only knob; the harness's own catalog provides the tools at that level. **`Tools []string` deletes from the proposed `ExecuteRequest`.** `findTool` and any future tool extensions belong inside ddx-agent's tool catalog; DDx never wraps or aliases tools.
.claude/skills/docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:126:ddx-agent has three capabilities the cloud-vendor harnesses don't: model catalog, provider discovery, route inspection. These don't fit the per-execution `Run` shape — they're queries against the agent's runtime knowledge. Expose them as additional methods on the same `Service`:
.claude/skills/docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:162:Six methods. `Execute` is the primary verb (mirrors the harness invocation); the other five are the "extras" only ddx-agent has — model catalog, discovery, route inspection.
.claude/skills/docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:164:DDx's `agentHarness` implementation of the `Harness` interface delegates `Run` → `Service.ExecuteStream`. DDx commands that need the extras (`ddx agent route-status`, `ddx agent providers`, `ddx agent models`, `ddx agent check`) call the corresponding `Service` method directly — they don't go through the harness abstraction since they're not invocations.
.claude/skills/docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:196:2. **Implement `Service` over the existing internals.** Wire the existing agent loop behind the new interface. Existing `cmd/ddx-agent/main.go` consumes the new `Service` (proves the interface is sufficient).
.claude/skills/docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:216:## Call-site mapping (every DDx file that imports ddx-agent)
.claude/skills/docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:233:| `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go` | The big one. Constructs providers (`oai.New`, `anthropic.New`, `virtual.New`), constructs compactor (`compaction.NewCompactor(embeddedCompactionConfig(...))`), registers tools (`tool.BashTool`, `tool.EditTool`, …, the `findTool` wrapper), builds prompt (`prompt.NewMetaPromptInjectorWithPaths`, `prompt.NewFromPreset`), opens session log (`session.NewLogger`), drives the loop via `agentlib.Run`, listens to `agentlib.Event` (compaction-end inspection for stall/breaker logic at lines 200-318) | Becomes the `agentHarness` impl of DDx's `Harness` interface. Body shrinks to: build `ExecuteRequest{Prompt, Model, WorkDir, Permissions, Effort, Timeout, StallPolicy}`, call `service.Execute(ctx, req)`, drain events. **All of:** provider construction, compactor wiring, tool registration, prompt building, session opening — moves into agentlib internals. The stall/circuit-breaker logic at 200-318 needs an upstream home — see "open structural decisions" below. |
.claude/skills/docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:234:| `cli/internal/agent/runner.go` | `Runner` struct exposes `AgentProvider interface{}` (test injection seam — line 36) and `CompactorOverride func(...)` (test compactor seam — line 53) | Drop `CompactorOverride` (compaction is internal). Replace `AgentProvider interface{}` with `Options.FakeProvider` test seam at the agentlib root — first-class part of the design, not hand-waved. |
.claude/skills/docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:235:| `cli/internal/agent/provider_deadline.go` | Wraps `agentlib.Provider` with per-request and idle-timeout timeouts; implements `StreamingProvider` too | Deletes. Move semantics into agentlib (it already owns provider construction). Caller passes `ExecuteRequest.Timeout` and optionally `ExecuteRequest.IdleTimeout`. |
.claude/skills/docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:236:| `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go` | `agentconfig.Load(workDir)`, exposes `Config agentconfig.ProviderConfig` for live `/v1/models` probing | Service owns discovery (`ListModels` is live-by-default per the principle). DDx-side discovery wrapper deletes; callers use `service.ListModels`. |
.claude/skills/docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:237:| `cli/internal/agent/providerstatus/probe.go` | `Probe(ctx, agentconfig.ProviderConfig)` calls `oai.DiscoverModels(...)` | Replaced by `service.HealthCheck(ctx, provider)`. The `providerstatus` package can stay as a DDx-side type for the result, sourced from the service. |
.claude/skills/docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:264:- **FEAT-006 — Agent Service** — DDx owns harness orchestration + cross-harness routing; embedded agent owns provider/backend selection.
.claude/skills/docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:271:DDx side, all in `cli/internal/agent/`:
.claude/skills/docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:286:| `cmd/ddx-agent/routing_provider.go:38` `newRouteProvider` | Per-route provider wrapper with candidate failover |
.claude/skills/docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:287:| `cmd/ddx-agent/routing_smart.go:28` smartRouteHistory/Candidate/Plan | Runtime scoring: reliability, performance, cost, capability |
.claude/skills/docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:288:| `cmd/ddx-agent/routing_scorer.go` `CandidateScorer` interface | Hook point so DDx can overlay quota/cost (Option-A pattern) |
.claude/skills/docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:319:## Decision: Option C — consolidate harnesses down into ddx-agent
.claude/skills/docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:321:### What ddx-agent becomes
.claude/skills/docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:329:**The product:** ddx-agent is the one stop shop for optimally routed one-shot noninteractive agentic prompts. DDx is one consumer (the bead-driven workflow); HELIX, Dun, and standalone CLI users are others.
.claude/skills/docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:335:2. **Two-role coherence.** ddx-agent's value prop ("optimally routed one-shot noninteractive prompts") only makes sense if it owns the routing **across** harnesses, not just within in-process providers. A consumer that wants "give me the best harness/provider/model for this prompt under cost constraint X" needs one entrypoint that knows about all options. Under B, that consumer has to glue DDx's harness routing to ddx-agent's provider routing themselves.
.claude/skills/docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:358:- `docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-006-agent-service.md` — rewrite to one page: "DDx invokes LLMs via the ddx-agent contract. DDx-side responsibilities: bead-driven invocation, execute-bead orchestration, evidence/session capture. End." No harness, routing, or provider language survives.
.claude/skills/docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:371:`docs/helix/02-design/contracts/CONTRACT-001-ddx-agent-service.md`:
.claude/skills/docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:386:Any remaining reference is a re-entanglement risk. Remove with prejudice. Code-side imports of `cli/internal/agent` types from outside DDx (if any — see HELIX/Dun audit below) get the same treatment.
.claude/skills/docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:390:When DDx needs new contract behavior, it files an issue/bead against ddx-agent referencing the use case. ddx-agent maintainers decide. DDx does not edit ddx-agent's internal specs and does not maintain a parallel description of agent's behavior. **One contract; one direction of dependency.**

.claude/skills/docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:477:**B. Lift all routing to DDx.** ddx-agent becomes a mechanism (list/probe/resolve/execute). DDx ranks `(harness, provider?, model)` tuples uniformly. Single-layer routing in DDx.
.claude/skills/docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:479:**C. Move harnesses DOWN into ddx-agent.** All "LLM routing" lives in one module — including subprocess wrappers for claude/codex/opencode/pi/gemini. DDx becomes a thin client. ddx-agent owns the whole LLM-execution-and-routing surface.
.claude/skills/docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:481:**Decisive C-favoring argument: comparison suite.** A stated project goal is that ddx-agent's native harness should *outperform* the subprocess wrappers (claude/codex/opencode) on batch noninteractive tasks. Proving that requires a comparison suite that runs identical prompts through every harness implementation and measures latency, cost, success rate, output quality. Today that suite is split: harness invocation lives in DDx (`cli/internal/agent/compare.go`, `cli/internal/agent/benchmark.go`), routing lives in DDx, but the native agent runtime lives in ddx-agent. Under C, the comparison suite collapses into one module that can A/B test (`harness=claude` subprocess) vs (`harness=agent-native`, `provider=bragi`, `model=qwen3.6`) with the same input/output schema and metrics collection. Under A or B, the suite still has to bridge two modules and measurement parity is harder to maintain.

docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:113:## Principle: uniform harness invocation shape, embedded transport for ddx-agent
docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:117:**ddx-agent should expose the same invocation shape, but stay in-process.** No subprocess required — the agent library is Go and DDx is Go. The reason to make the shape uniform isn't transport; it's so DDx can have a single `Harness` interface in its own code with multiple implementations:
docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:130:`RunRequest` carries: `Prompt`, `Model`, `WorkDir`, `Permissions`, `Effort`, `Timeout`, `Metadata`. Nothing else. Tools are not in `RunRequest` — claude doesn't take a tools flag, codex doesn't, ddx-agent won't.
docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:134:Tools are entirely internal to whichever harness is running. `claude` does not let DDx pick its tools; `codex` does not; `ddx-agent` won't either. Permission level is the only knob; the harness's own catalog provides the tools at that level. **`Tools []string` deletes from the proposed `ExecuteRequest`.** `findTool` and any future tool extensions belong inside ddx-agent's tool catalog; DDx never wraps or aliases tools.
docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:138:ddx-agent has three capabilities the cloud-vendor harnesses don't: model catalog, provider discovery, route inspection. These don't fit the per-execution `Run` shape — they're queries against the agent's runtime knowledge. Expose them as additional methods on the same `Service`:
docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:174:Six methods. `Execute` is the primary verb (mirrors the harness invocation); the other five are the "extras" only ddx-agent has — model catalog, discovery, route inspection.
docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:212:2. **Implement `Service` over the existing internals.** Wire the existing agent loop behind the new interface. Existing `cmd/ddx-agent/main.go` consumes the new `Service` (proves the interface is sufficient).
docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:232:## Call-site mapping (every DDx file that imports ddx-agent)
docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:249:| `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go` | The big one. Constructs providers (`oai.New`, `anthropic.New`, `virtual.New`), constructs compactor (`compaction.NewCompactor(embeddedCompactionConfig(...))`), registers tools (`tool.BashTool`, `tool.EditTool`, …, the `findTool` wrapper), builds prompt (`prompt.NewMetaPromptInjectorWithPaths`, `prompt.NewFromPreset`), opens session log (`session.NewLogger`), drives the loop via `agentlib.Run`, listens to `agentlib.Event` (compaction-end inspection for stall/breaker logic at lines 200-318) | Becomes the `agentHarness` impl of DDx's `Harness` interface. Body shrinks to: build `ExecuteRequest{Prompt, Model, WorkDir, Permissions, Effort, Timeout, StallPolicy}`, call `service.Execute(ctx, req)`, drain events. **All of:** provider construction, compactor wiring, tool registration, prompt building, session opening — moves into agentlib internals. The stall/circuit-breaker logic at 200-318 needs an upstream home — see "open structural decisions" below. |
docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:250:| `cli/internal/agent/runner.go` | `Runner` struct exposes `AgentProvider interface{}` (test injection seam — line 36) and `CompactorOverride func(...)` (test compactor seam — line 53) | Drop `CompactorOverride` (compaction is internal). Replace `AgentProvider interface{}` with `Options.FakeProvider` test seam at the agentlib root — first-class part of the design, not hand-waved. |
docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:251:| `cli/internal/agent/provider_deadline.go` | Wraps `agentlib.Provider` with per-request and idle-timeout timeouts; implements `StreamingProvider` too | Deletes. Move semantics into agentlib (it already owns provider construction). Caller passes `ExecuteRequest.Timeout` and optionally `ExecuteRequest.IdleTimeout`. |
docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:252:| `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go` | `agentconfig.Load(workDir)`, exposes `Config agentconfig.ProviderConfig` for live `/v1/models` probing | Service owns discovery (`ListModels` is live-by-default per the principle). DDx-side discovery wrapper deletes; callers use `service.ListModels`. |
docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:253:| `cli/internal/agent/providerstatus/probe.go` | `Probe(ctx, agentconfig.ProviderConfig)` calls `oai.DiscoverModels(...)` | Replaced by `service.HealthCheck(ctx, provider)`. The `providerstatus` package can stay as a DDx-side type for the result, sourced from the service. |
docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:288:DDx side, all in `cli/internal/agent/`:
docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:303:| `cmd/ddx-agent/routing_provider.go:38` `newRouteProvider` | Per-route provider wrapper with candidate failover |
docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:304:| `cmd/ddx-agent/routing_smart.go:28` smartRouteHistory/Candidate/Plan | Runtime scoring: reliability, performance, cost, capability |
docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:305:| `cmd/ddx-agent/routing_scorer.go` `CandidateScorer` interface | Hook point so DDx can overlay quota/cost (Option-A pattern) |
docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:336:## Decision: Option C — consolidate harnesses down into ddx-agent
docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:383:- `docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-006-agent-service.md` — rewrite to one page: "DDx invokes LLMs via the ddx-agent contract. DDx-side responsibilities: bead-driven invocation, execute-bead orchestration, evidence/session capture. End." DDx may keep copied/linkable evidence-envelope references and forward Fizeau transcript `ServiceEvent`s as opaque evidence, but it is only a pass-through/marshalling consumer for those events. Fizeau owns transcript, session-log, and progress rendering plus transcript semantics. DDx must never render, rehydrate, parse, or semantically interpret inner Fizeau transcript or session-log content; it only preserves the opaque envelope and attachments.
docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:396:`docs/helix/02-design/contracts/CONTRACT-001-ddx-agent-service.md`:
docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:411:Any remaining reference is a re-entanglement risk. Remove with prejudice. Code-side imports of `cli/internal/agent` types from outside DDx (if any — see HELIX and other consumers audit below) get the same treatment.
docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:415:When DDx needs new contract behavior, it files an issue/bead against ddx-agent referencing the use case. ddx-agent maintainers decide. DDx does not edit ddx-agent's internal specs and does not maintain a parallel description of agent's behavior. **One contract; one direction of dependency.**

docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:431:| `cli/internal/agent/routing.go` | **Moves to ddx-agent**, merges with `cmd/ddx-agent/routing_smart.go` + `routing_provider.go` into one routing engine |

docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:435:| `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go`, `runner.go` | **Deletes**; replaced by Service.Execute calls |
docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:436:| `cli/internal/agent/provider_deadline.go` | **Moves to ddx-agent** (deadlines are internal to execution) |
docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:437:| `cli/internal/agent/types.go` (RouteRequest, CandidatePlan, HarnessState, Harness) | **Moves to ddx-agent** as internal types |
docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:438:| `cli/internal/agent/compare.go`, `benchmark.go`, `quorum.go`, `condense.go` | **Move to ddx-agent** — these ARE the comparison suite the user wants in one module |
docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:439:| `cli/internal/agent/providerstatus/probe.go` | **Moves to ddx-agent** |
docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:440:| `cli/internal/agent/claude_stream.go`, `claude_quota_cache.go` | **Move to ddx-agent** as part of the claude harness implementation |
docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:441:| `cli/internal/agent/jsonl.go`, `format.go`, `session_log_format.go`, `session_log_tailer.go` | **Mostly move** to ddx-agent (event/format primitives); DDx keeps only copied/linkable evidence-envelope metadata and attachment pointers, and it does not render, rehydrate, or parse the inner Fizeau transcript or session-log content. Fizeau owns the session-log and progress rendering surfaces plus transcript semantics; DDx is only an opaque forwarder for those artifacts |
docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:442:| `cli/internal/agent/state.go`, `executions_mirror.go`, `executor.go`, `script.go` | **Stay** in DDx — these are execute-bead orchestration (DDx-specific concern) |
docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:443:| `cli/internal/agent/grade.go`, `pricing.go`, `models.go`, `catalog.go`, `model_catalog_yaml.go`, `agent_catalog_shared.go`, `worktree_skills.go` | Mixed — likely most move; some DDx-specific glue stays |
docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:500:**A. Routing stays in ddx-agent, exposed via a candidate-ranking API.** DDx calls in to score candidates for harness routing. Two-layer routing where the inner layer asks the agent.
docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:502:**B. Lift all routing to DDx.** ddx-agent becomes a mechanism (list/probe/resolve/execute). DDx ranks `(harness, provider?, model)` tuples uniformly. Single-layer routing in DDx.
docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:504:**C. Move harnesses DOWN into ddx-agent.** All "LLM routing" lives in one module — including subprocess wrappers for claude/codex/opencode/pi/gemini. DDx becomes a thin client. ddx-agent owns the whole LLM-execution-and-routing surface.
docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:506:**Decisive C-favoring argument: comparison suite.** A stated project goal is that ddx-agent's native harness should *outperform* the subprocess wrappers (claude/codex/opencode) on batch noninteractive tasks. Proving that requires a comparison suite that runs identical prompts through every harness implementation and measures latency, cost, success rate, output quality. Today that suite is split: harness invocation lives in DDx (`cli/internal/agent/compare.go`, `cli/internal/agent/benchmark.go`), routing lives in DDx, but the native agent runtime lives in ddx-agent. Under C, the comparison suite collapses into one module that can A/B test (`harness=claude` subprocess) vs (`harness=agent-native`, `provider=bragi`, `model=qwen3.6`) with the same input/output schema and metrics collection. Under A or B, the suite still has to bridge two modules and measurement parity is harder to maintain.
docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:510:| Algorithm location | ddx-agent (exposed) | DDx | ddx-agent (internal) |

.agents/skills/docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:101:## Principle: uniform harness invocation shape, embedded transport for ddx-agent
.agents/skills/docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:105:**ddx-agent should expose the same invocation shape, but stay in-process.** No subprocess required — the agent library is Go and DDx is Go. The reason to make the shape uniform isn't transport; it's so DDx can have a single `Harness` interface in its own code with multiple implementations:
.agents/skills/docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:118:`RunRequest` carries: `Prompt`, `Model`, `WorkDir`, `Permissions`, `Effort`, `Timeout`, `Metadata`. Nothing else. Tools are not in `RunRequest` — claude doesn't take a tools flag, codex doesn't, ddx-agent won't.
.agents/skills/docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:122:Tools are entirely internal to whichever harness is running. `claude` does not let DDx pick its tools; `codex` does not; `ddx-agent` won't either. Permission level is the only knob; the harness's own catalog provides the tools at that level. **`Tools []string` deletes from the proposed `ExecuteRequest`.** `findTool` and any future tool extensions belong inside ddx-agent's tool catalog; DDx never wraps or aliases tools.
.agents/skills/docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:126:ddx-agent has three capabilities the cloud-vendor harnesses don't: model catalog, provider discovery, route inspection. These don't fit the per-execution `Run` shape — they're queries against the agent's runtime knowledge. Expose them as additional methods on the same `Service`:
.agents/skills/docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:162:Six methods. `Execute` is the primary verb (mirrors the harness invocation); the other five are the "extras" only ddx-agent has — model catalog, discovery, route inspection.
.agents/skills/docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:164:DDx's `agentHarness` implementation of the `Harness` interface delegates `Run` → `Service.ExecuteStream`. DDx commands that need the extras (`ddx agent route-status`, `ddx agent providers`, `ddx agent models`, `ddx agent check`) call the corresponding `Service` method directly — they don't go through the harness abstraction since they're not invocations.
.agents/skills/docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:196:2. **Implement `Service` over the existing internals.** Wire the existing agent loop behind the new interface. Existing `cmd/ddx-agent/main.go` consumes the new `Service` (proves the interface is sufficient).
.agents/skills/docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:216:## Call-site mapping (every DDx file that imports ddx-agent)
.agents/skills/docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:233:| `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go` | The big one. Constructs providers (`oai.New`, `anthropic.New`, `virtual.New`), constructs compactor (`compaction.NewCompactor(embeddedCompactionConfig(...))`), registers tools (`tool.BashTool`, `tool.EditTool`, …, the `findTool` wrapper), builds prompt (`prompt.NewMetaPromptInjectorWithPaths`, `prompt.NewFromPreset`), opens session log (`session.NewLogger`), drives the loop via `agentlib.Run`, listens to `agentlib.Event` (compaction-end inspection for stall/breaker logic at lines 200-318) | Becomes the `agentHarness` impl of DDx's `Harness` interface. Body shrinks to: build `ExecuteRequest{Prompt, Model, WorkDir, Permissions, Effort, Timeout, StallPolicy}`, call `service.Execute(ctx, req)`, drain events. **All of:** provider construction, compactor wiring, tool registration, prompt building, session opening — moves into agentlib internals. The stall/circuit-breaker logic at 200-318 needs an upstream home — see "open structural decisions" below. |
.agents/skills/docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:234:| `cli/internal/agent/runner.go` | `Runner` struct exposes `AgentProvider interface{}` (test injection seam — line 36) and `CompactorOverride func(...)` (test compactor seam — line 53) | Drop `CompactorOverride` (compaction is internal). Replace `AgentProvider interface{}` with `Options.FakeProvider` test seam at the agentlib root — first-class part of the design, not hand-waved. |
.agents/skills/docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:235:| `cli/internal/agent/provider_deadline.go` | Wraps `agentlib.Provider` with per-request and idle-timeout timeouts; implements `StreamingProvider` too | Deletes. Move semantics into agentlib (it already owns provider construction). Caller passes `ExecuteRequest.Timeout` and optionally `ExecuteRequest.IdleTimeout`. |
.agents/skills/docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:236:| `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go` | `agentconfig.Load(workDir)`, exposes `Config agentconfig.ProviderConfig` for live `/v1/models` probing | Service owns discovery (`ListModels` is live-by-default per the principle). DDx-side discovery wrapper deletes; callers use `service.ListModels`. |

.agents/skills/docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:319:## Decision: Option C — consolidate harnesses down into ddx-agent
.agents/skills/docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:321:### What ddx-agent becomes
.agents/skills/docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:329:**The product:** ddx-agent is the one stop shop for optimally routed one-shot noninteractive agentic prompts. DDx is one consumer (the bead-driven workflow); HELIX, Dun, and standalone CLI users are others.
.agents/skills/docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:335:2. **Two-role coherence.** ddx-agent's value prop ("optimally routed one-shot noninteractive prompts") only makes sense if it owns the routing **across** harnesses, not just within in-process providers. A consumer that wants "give me the best harness/provider/model for this prompt under cost constraint X" needs one entrypoint that knows about all options. Under B, that consumer has to glue DDx's harness routing to ddx-agent's provider routing themselves.
.agents/skills/docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:358:- `docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-006-agent-service.md` — rewrite to one page: "DDx invokes LLMs via the ddx-agent contract. DDx-side responsibilities: bead-driven invocation, execute-bead orchestration, evidence/session capture. End." No harness, routing, or provider language survives.

DDx does not edit ddx-agent's internal specs and does not maintain a parallel description of agent's behavior. **One contract; one direction of dependency.**

.agents/skills/docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:475:**A. Routing stays in ddx-agent, exposed via a candidate-ranking API.** DDx calls in to score candidates for harness routing. Two-layer routing where the inner layer asks the agent.
.agents/skills/docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:477:**B. Lift all routing to DDx.** ddx-agent becomes a mechanism (list/probe/resolve/execute). DDx ranks `(harness, provider?, model)` tuples uniformly. Single-layer routing in DDx.
.agents/skills/docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:479:**C. Move harnesses DOWN into ddx-agent.** All "LLM routing" lives in one module — including subprocess wrappers for claude/codex/opencode/pi/gemini. DDx becomes a thin client. ddx-agent owns the whole LLM-execution-and-routing surface.
.agents/skills/docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-18-ddx-agent-service-interface.md:481:**Decisive C-favoring argument: comparison suite.** A stated project goal is that ddx-agent's native harness should *outperform* the subprocess wrappers (claude/codex/opencode) on batch noninteractive tasks. Proving that requires a comparison suite that runs identical prompts through every harness implementation and measures latency, cost, success rate, output quality. Today that suite is split: harness invocation lives in DDx (`cli/internal/agent/compare.go`, `cli/internal/agent/benchmark.go`), routing lives in DDx, but the native agent runtime lives in ddx-agent. Under C, the comparison suite collapses into one module that can A/B test (`harness=claude` subprocess) vs (`harness=agent-native`, `provider=bragi`, `model=qwen3.6`) with the same input/output schema and metrics collection. Under A or B, the suite still has to bridge two modules and measurement parity is harder to maintain.

+The `ddx` CLI is a single Go binary providing all DDx platform services locally: document library management, bead tracker, task execution dispatch, execution definitions and runs, document dependency graph, persona composition, template application, git sync, and meta-prompt injection.
 
 ## Requirements
 
@@ -51,16 +51,17 @@ The `ddx` CLI is a single Go binary providing all DDx platform services locally:
 18. Configurable ID prefix (auto-detected from repo name)
 19. Backend abstraction (jsonl/bd/br)
 
-**Agent Service (implemented — FEAT-006)**
-20. `ddx agent run --profile=<default|cheap|fast|smart> --prompt <file>` — invoke an AI agent through automatic routing
-20b. `ddx agent run --model <ref-or-exact> [--effort <level>]` — request a specific model ref, alias, or exact model pin
-20c. `ddx agent run --harness=<name>` — explicitly override automatic routing and force one harness
-21. `ddx agent run --quorum=majority --harnesses=a,b` — multi-agent consensus
-22. `ddx agent list` — show available harnesses with availability status
-23. `ddx agent doctor` — harness health and routing-readiness check (installed, reachable, authenticated, quota state, degradation)
-24. `ddx agent log [session-id]` — session history with token tracking
-25. `ddx agent capabilities [harness]` — show reasoning levels, exact-pin support, and effective profile/model mappings for a harness
-25b. `ddx agent usage [--since] [--harness] [--format]` — token and cost consumption summary (FEAT-014)
+**Task Execution (top-level — FEAT-006/FEAT-010)**
+20. `ddx run --min-power <n> [--max-power <n>] --prompt <file>` — layer-1: invoke Fizeau with requested abstract power bounds; Fizeau owns model/provider routing
+20b. `ddx run --top-power --prompt <file>` — request a `MinPower` threshold derived from Fizeau's available model/power catalog
+20c. `ddx run --effort <level>` — pass non-routing reasoning/effort intent to Fizeau
+20c-1. `ddx run --harness <name> --provider <name> --model <name>` — optional passthrough constraints sent unchanged to Fizeau; DDx does not validate, resolve, fallback, branch on, or widen them
+20d. `ddx try <bead> [--from <rev>] [--no-merge]` — layer-2: bead attempt in isolated worktree with merge-or-preserve semantics
+20e. `ddx work` — layer-3: drain the bead execution queue (mechanical drain; supervisory decisions stay in plugins/HELIX)
+21. `ddx fizeau providers` — show available providers with live status
+22. `ddx fizeau models [--provider <name>]` — show models for a configured provider
+23. `ddx fizeau route-status` — show routing table, cooldowns, and recent decisions
+24. `ddx fizeau check` — probe provider runtime availability (liveness, model inventory)
 
 **Execution Service (in progress — FEAT-010)**
 26. `ddx exec list [--artifact ID]` — list execution definitions

This is the primary operator-facing surface for draining the bead execution queue; `ddx agent execute-loop` remains available for scripts and backward compatibility.

 **Embedded Utilities**
 47. `ddx jq <filter> [file...]` — embedded jq processor (powered by gojq), eliminating external jq dependency for HELIX and other workflow tools. Supports standard jq flags: `-r`, `-c`, `-s`, `-n`, `-R`, `-e`, `-j`, `-S`, `--tab`, `--indent`, `--arg`, `--argjson`, `--slurpfile`. Reads from stdin or file arguments. Pure Go, no CGo.

+44. Core skills: `ddx-bead` (guided bead create/triage), `ddx` (guided task execution with `run` / `try` / `work` and Fizeau passthrough constraints), `ddx-install` (guided package installation)

data.output:
**Task Execution Boundary (FEAT-006/FEAT-010)**

DDx owns the public workflow verbs `ddx run`, `ddx try`, and `ddx work`. Fizeau
owns routing, provider/model discovery, and concrete route selection. DDx does
not mount `ddx agent`, does not provide aliases for it, and does not keep any
legacy workflow verbs under that namespace.

22. Fizeau diagnostics, if exposed, remain read-only and separate from the
    workflow verbs; DDx may surface them only as Fizeau-owned observability, not

---
27. `ddx exec show <id>` — show one execution definition
28. `ddx exec validate <id>` — validate an execution definition and its linked artifacts
29. `ddx exec run <id>` — execute a definition and persist an immutable run record
30. `ddx exec log <run-id>` — show captured raw logs for one run
31. `ddx exec result <run-id>` — show structured result data for one run
32. `ddx exec history [--artifact ID] [--definition ID]` — inspect historical runs
32b. `ddx exec define <name> --artifact <id> --command <cmd>` — create an execution definition
32c. `ddx metric validate/run/history/compare/trend` — metric projections over execution substrate
